meanings: Animal with black and white stripes
A bird that sees at night
A bird that sees in day
A vegetable that makes you cry
We rest our head on it

grammar.c: A noun is a naming word.
Nouns name people, places, animals, things.
Proper nouns start with capital letters.
Name words tell us who or what.
A verb is a doing word.
Action words tell what we do.
Describing words tell how things look.
Describing words tell more about nouns.
grammar.q: Find describing word: The red rose

grammar.c: Describing words say how things look or taste.

grammar.q: Fill: ___ egg is on the table.

grammar.c: In, on, under are position words.
Add -es to words ending in x.
One is singular, more is plural.
Sentences start with capital, end with full stop.
Pronouns replace nouns: he, she, it, they.
grammar.q: Ravi is a boy. ___ plays cricket.

grammar.c: It replaces a thing or animal.

grammar.q: Ram and I study. ___ are friends.

grammar.c: Rhyming words sound alike at the end.
Words ending with same sound rhyme.
Vowels are A, E, I, O, U.
Every word has at least one vowel.
Boy is masculine, Girl is feminine.
He is masculine, She is feminine.
These = near many, Those = far many.
I am, He/She is, They are.
He is, She is, It is.
We are, They are, You are.
He has, I have, They have.
She has, He has, It has.
First word of sentence is capital.

grammar.q: What do we say when we want help?
How many days in a week?
How many months in a year?

grammar.c: Basic colours: red, blue, green, yellow.
Leaves are green, sky is blue.

grammar.q: How many sides does a triangle have?

grammar.c: Eyes, ears, nose, mouth, hands, legs.
Pet: dog, cat. Wild: lion, tiger.
We wear warm clothes in winter.
5 senses: see, hear, smell, taste, touch.

syllabus.c: A noun is a naming word.
A verb is a doing word.
Sentences start with capital, end with full stop.
Pronouns replace nouns: he, she, it, they.
syllabus.q: Ravi is a boy. ___ plays cricket.

syllabus.c: Rhyming words sound alike at the end.
Vowels are A, E, I, O, U.
Boy is masculine, Girl is feminine.
I am, He/She is, They are.
He has, I have, They have.

sentences.s: The cat sat on the mat.
The bird sits on the tree.
We go to bed at night.
